title: Astronauts Return Safely from Space Adventure
summary: A new video shows a happy moment when a recovery crew opened the hatch of the Orion capsule after it landed in the sea. The four astronauts inside—Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen—were all safe and well. They had traveled further from Earth than any humans before, making this a historic mission. The capsule splashed down on April 10, and everyone cheered when the astronauts were found to be okay. This event is part of the Artemis II mission, which aims to explore space and learn more about our universe. The astronauts are now back home, and scientists will study their journey to help plan future space trips.

background_read: The Artemis II mission is a space exploration program by NASA to send astronauts around the moon and back. It helps scientists learn how to live and work in deep space for future missions to Mars. Astronauts train for years to go on these trips, and they use special capsules like Orion to keep them safe. When they return to Earth, they land in the ocean, and teams of experts help them get out and check their health. This mission is important because it tests new technology and prepares for longer space journeys.
Article_Structure: WHO: The four astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen, and the recovery crew.
WHAT: A recovery crew opened the hatch of the Orion capsule after it landed in the sea, and the astronauts were found safe and well.
WHERE: The Orion capsule splashed down in the ocean, and the event was captured on video.
WHY: This was part of the historic Artemis II mission to explore space and test new technology for future trips.

perspectives.description: This mission shows how humans can travel further into space than ever before, helping us learn more about the universe.
The successful recovery of the astronauts highlights the importance of careful planning and teamwork in space missions.
